content: Antibiotics are medicines that help us fight germs that make us sick. But sometimes, germs learn how to protect themselves from antibiotics, and this is called antibiotic resistance. Germs can change little parts of themselves so the medicine doesn’t work anymore. This happens a lot when people don’t finish their medicine or use antibiotics when they don’t need them. Also, when animals on farms get antibiotics too much, germs there can become resistant and spread to people. To help stop antibiotic resistance, we should only use antibiotics when a doctor says we need them. Washing our hands and staying healthy can also keep germs away so we don’t need antibiotics as much. If we all do these things, antibiotics will keep working to help us stay healthy for a long time.
